target_question: Does a frozen bathymetry/backscatter representation generalize to a direct, independently collected three-class seafloor endpoint across whole source groups?
admission_requirements: The endpoint is a direct measurement or prospectively collected visual observation rather than a mapped interpretation, suitability model, or derivative of the candidate inputs.
The endpoint footprint overlaps an admitted exact raster footprint and every paired observation has auditable time, coordinates, measurement identity, and source lineage.

Collection and labeling are independent of candidate model outputs and do not reuse a source family already exposed during representation development unless a new protocol explicitly treats it as exploratory.
The endpoint schema, class mapping, grouping unit, spatial buffer, support floor, exclusions, and decision rule are frozen before labels or class support are read.

Every frozen class has the preregistered minimum support on both sides of at least one whole-source-group partition; adjacent rows, tracks, cruises, sites, deployments, or source programs cannot cross the partition boundary.
Exact licensing, permitted use, source accuracy, uncertainty, and data-custody reviews pass before any raster pairing or model access.

reviewed_candidates.title: NOAA Digital Coast Benthic Grab Samples
reviewed_candidates.geographic_relation: The published inventory contains five East Coast data collections and no San Francisco or Santa Barbara collection.
reviewed_candidates.lineage_relation: Independent measurement mechanism, but absent from the target geography and structurally unable to sample hard substrate.

reviewed_candidates.title: San Francisco Bay Benthic Habitat 2011
reviewed_candidates.geographic_relation: Mapped San Francisco Bay benthic cover does not establish direct endpoint coverage inside the admitted offshore state-waters raster footprint.
reviewed_candidates.lineage_relation: The catalog class is a mapped product derived primarily from remote imagery or acoustic surveys, not a new direct independently collected endpoint.

reviewed_candidates.title: Benthic substrate type off California
reviewed_candidates.steward: NOAA National Centers for Coastal Ocean Science
reviewed_candidates.geographic_relation: The broad California polygon layer includes southern California but has variable spatial resolution and is not an exact direct-observation footprint.
reviewed_candidates.lineage_relation: A Moss Landing compilation revised with digitized hard-bottom maps from UCSB and the Minerals Management Service; NOAA states that the map has not been field validated.

reviewed_candidates.title: California halibut habitat suitability model for Channel Islands National Marine Sanctuary
reviewed_candidates.steward: NOAA National Centers for Coastal Ocean Science
reviewed_candidates.geographic_relation: The modeled southern California extent overlaps the broader product region but does not provide direct seafloor observations.
reviewed_candidates.lineage_relation: Suitability classes were calculated from literature associations and combined bathymetry and benthic-substrate GIS layers; NOAA states that the suitability values have not been validated.

reviewed_candidates.title: USGS Data Series 781 video observations
reviewed_candidates.geographic_relation: Direct observations overlap portions of San Francisco and the Santa Barbara South Coast admitted imagery.
reviewed_candidates.lineage_relation: This is the already audited DS781 source family; whole-track, whole-cruise, and residual statewide screens do not distribute every frozen class across separable valid groups.

reviewed_candidates.title: USGS Data Series 182 Pacific Coast sediment database
reviewed_candidates.geographic_relation: Direct sediment records intersect both admitted regions, but valid local observations are sparse and source-concentrated.
reviewed_candidates.lineage_relation: This is the already audited DS182 source family; the San Francisco endpoint has zero valid rows and the Santa Barbara endpoint has no gravel-bearing rows and no eligible whole-source partition.

reviewed_candidates.title: USGS Data Series 781 seafloor-character and habitat interpretations
reviewed_candidates.geographic_relation: Mapped habitat products cover admitted San Francisco and Santa Barbara imagery.
reviewed_candidates.lineage_relation: The interpretations use bathymetry, backscatter, and hillshade as primary sources, with DS781 video and DS182 sediment as supporting observations.

next_admissible_path: Freeze and externally timestamp a prospective direct-video collection protocol before site assignments or labels exist; keep the candidate representations and their outputs hidden from collection and labeling, preserve whole-deployment groups, and require fixed three-class support on both sides of a whole-group partition before raster pairing or model access.
